description: Add tabState='ready' to BrowserToolsCard's PluginIcon so the green dot shows by default, since browser tools are always available when the card is visible

userStories.title: Pass tabState='ready' to PluginIcon in BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: As a user, I want the browser tools Chrome icon to show the green ready dot by default, since browser tools are always available whenever the extension is connected to the server and the card is visible
acceptanceCriteria: The PluginIcon in BrowserToolsCard.tsx receives `tabState="ready"` as a prop
The green `bg-success` status dot is visible at the bottom-right corner of the Chrome icon whenever the BrowserToolsCard is rendered
userStories.notes:
This is a one-line change in `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx`.

At line 118-124, the `<PluginIcon>` render currently has no `tabState` prop, so it defaults to `'closed'` (which renders no status dot). Add `tabState="ready"`:

```tsx
<PluginIcon
  pluginName="browser"
  displayName="Browser"
  tabState="ready"
  size={32}
  iconSvg={CHROME_ICON_SVG}
  iconInactiveSvg={CHROME_ICON_SVG}
/>
```

Browser tools are built-in and always available when the extension is connected. The BrowserToolsCard is only rendered when connected (App.tsx only shows plugin content when `connected && !loading`), so `tabState="ready"` is always correct.

**IMPORTANT for commit message:** This change is intentionally separate from but compatible with the `prd-activity-indicator-dots` PRD which also modifies the same PluginIcon render in BrowserToolsCard.tsx (to add an `active` prop). If there is a merge conflict, the correct resolution is to keep BOTH changes — `tabState="ready"` from this PRD AND `active={hasActiveTool}` from the activity-indicator PRD. The final line should have both props.
